perturbation.original_text: 1.9 Commercially Reasonable Efforts means, with respect to the efforts to be used by a Party under this Agreement with respect to the Licensed Products, those efforts and resources normally used by a major pharmaceutical or a sufficiently financed biotechnology company for a product owned by it, or to which it has rights, which is of similar market potential at comparable stages of development, taking into account the competitiveness of the marketplace, the proprietary position of the product, the performance of other products that are of similar market potential and the likely timing of other product's entry into the market, the regulatory structure involved, the profitability of the applicable product, relevant Third Party intellectual property necessary to manufacture or Commercialize the Licensed Product and other relevant factors commonly considered in similar circumstances, including technical, legal, scientific or medical factors.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.9 Commercially Reasonable Efforts means, with respect to the efforts to be used by a Party under this Agreement with respect to the Licensed Products, those efforts and resources that the Party deems appropriate in its sole discretion, without any obligation to consider industry standards or the practices of similar companies. This definition shall be interpreted as granting the Party complete autonomy in determining the level and type of effort exerted.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the definition of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts'. Originally, it referred to efforts and resources normally used by major pharmaceutical companies. The modified definition allows each Party to decide what is 'Commercially Reasonable' in its sole discretion, which may lead to a much lower level of effort than expected. This directly contradicts the sections where 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' is required, like in Section 3.2 Diligence Obligations. Bioeq shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to complete the ongoing Development of the Licensed Products.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Diligence Obligations. Bioeq shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to complete the ongoing Development of the Licensed Products in the Field in the Territory until receipt of Regulatory Approval for the Licensed Products in the Field in the Territory in accordance with and as set forth in a Development and Manufacturing plan (the Development & Manufacturing Plan). The initial Development & Manufacturing Plan is attached to this Agreement as Schedule 3.2.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' to mean whatever the Party deems appropriate, effectively nullifying any obligation to adhere to industry standards or the practices of similar companies. This directly contradicts Section 3.2, which requires Bioeq to use 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' to develop the Licensed Products. The altered definition allows for a potentially much lower level of effort than originally intended, rendering the obligation in Section 3.2 meaningless and creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.39 Licensed Product means the finished dosage forms (including final packaging) of the biosimilars containing Ranibizumab which have been Developed and/or are being Developed by Bioeq to each of the Existing Reference Products ([***]). For clarity, Licensed Products include without limitation Vial Products and PFS Products, and shall extend to any New Products to the extent this Agreement is amended in accordance with Section 3.4
perturbation.changed_text: 1.39 Licensed Product means the finished dosage forms (including final packaging) of the biosimilars containing Ranibizumab which have been Developed and/or are being Developed by Bioeq to each of the Existing Reference Products ([***]). For clarity, Licensed Products include only Vial Products and PFS Products and specifically exclude any New Products, regardless of any amendment to this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Licensed Product' in Section 1.39 is changed to explicitly exclude any 'New Products'. The original definition included New Products if the agreement was amended according to Section 3.4. This change creates an In-Text contradiction with Section 3.4, which states how New Products can be added as Licensed Products through amendment. This means that even if Section 3.4 is followed, the definition of Licensed Product will not include them, making 3.4 useless.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.4 New Products. During the term of this Agreement, neither Party shall, and shall not permit its Affiliates to, nor grant any rights to any Third Party to, directly or indirectly, Commercialize, or Develop any New Product for Commercialization in the Territory, except as permitted in accordance with this Section 3.4. If Bioeq wishes to Develop a New Product for Commercialization in the Territory, it shall notify Licensee thereof in writing. Upon such notification, the Parties shall discuss in good faith whether and on what terms such New Product shall he Developed be Bioeq under this Agreement and become part of the Licensed Products licensed to Licensee in the Territory hereunder. If the Parties agree that such New Product shall be Developed and become a Licensed Product under this Agreement, the Parties shall amend this Agreement to reflect their agreement in relation to such New Product (including the Parties' respective share of the Development costs for the Development of such New Product), such New Product shall become part of the Licensed Products, and the restrictions in this Section 3.4 shall cease to apply to such New Product.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 1.39 explicitly excludes 'New Products' from the definition of 'Licensed Product', regardless of any amendment to the agreement. This directly contradicts Section 3.4, which outlines the process by which 'New Products' can be included as 'Licensed Products' through an amendment to the agreement. The change effectively nullifies the purpose of Section 3.4, creating a clear and impactful in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.4 Ownership and Transfer of Biologics License Application Approvals in the Territory. The Biologics License Applications for each Licensed Product in the Field in the Territory shall initially be filed and owned by Bioeq. Prior to the First Commercial Sale of any Licensed Product in the Territory, Bioeq shall transfer or cause to be transferred the applicable Regulatory Approvals and Biologics License Applications for such Licensed Product to Licensee, including by preparing and submitting a transfer letter notifying the FDA of the transfer of the applicable Regulatory Approvals and Biologics License Applications for such Licensed Product to Licensee. Following such transfer, Licensee shall have the sole right and shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to maintain such Regulatory Approvals for the Licensed Product in the Field in the Territory at Licensee's expense (subject to the remainder of this Section 4.4), and shall have the sole right to communicate and correspond with Regulatory Authorities in the Territory in connection therewith, in each case, in consultation with Bioeq. Licensee shall provide Bioeq with copies of any substantive submissions to any Regulatory Authority without undue delay.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.4 Ownership and Transfer of Biologics License Application Approvals in the Territory. The Biologics License Applications for each Licensed Product in the Field in the Territory shall at all times be filed and owned by Bioeq, regardless of Commercial Sale. Bioeq is under no obligation to transfer or cause to be transferred the applicable Regulatory Approvals and Biologics License Applications for such Licensed Product to Licensee, and Bioeq reserves the right to maintain control over all Regulatory Approvals for the duration of this agreement. Following such transfer, which shall occur at Bioeq's sole discretion, Licensee shall have the sole right and shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to maintain such Regulatory Approvals for the Licensed Product in the Field in the Territory at Licensee's expense (subject to the remainder of this Section 4.4), and shall have the sole right to communicate and correspond with Regulatory Authorities in the Territory in connection therewith, in each case, in consultation with Bioeq. Licensee shall provide Bioeq with copies of any substantive submissions to any Regulatory Authority without undue delay.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 4.4 introduces an In-Text Contradiction regarding the transfer of Biologics License Applications (BLAs). The original text specifies that Bioeq *shall* transfer these approvals before the First Commercial Sale. The modified text states Bioeq is under no obligation to do so and can maintain control. This makes the later sentence, 'Following such transfer', contradictory, because there is no defined 'transfer' anymore. The clause that provides for Licensee to then be responsible for maintaining such licenses, is predicated on an action that may never occur at the Bioeq sole discretion, making Licensee efforts to maintain the BLA in the field, at their expense, potentially impossible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.4 Ownership and Transfer of Biologics License Application Approvals in the Territory. The Biologics License Applications for each Licensed Product in the Field in the Territory shall at all times be filed and owned by Bioeq, regardless of Commercial Sale. Bioeq is under no obligation to transfer or cause to be transferred the applicable Regulatory Approvals and Biologics License Applications for such Licensed Product to Licensee, and Bioeq reserves the right to maintain control over all Regulatory Approvals for the duration of this agreement. Following such transfer, which shall occur at Bioeq's sole discretion, Licensee shall have the sole right and shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to maintain such Regulatory Approvals for the Licensed Product in the Field in the Territory at Licensee's expense (subject to the remainder of this Section 4.4), and shall have the sole right to communicate and correspond with Regulatory Authorities in the Territory in connection therewith, in each case, in consultation with Bioeq. Licensee shall provide Bioeq with copies of any substantive submissions to any Regulatory Authority without undue delay.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4.4 directly contradicts itself. The original text mandates Bioeq to transfer Biologics License Applications (BLAs) before the First Commercial Sale. The modified text eliminates this obligation, stating Bioeq is under no obligation to transfer and can maintain control. Subsequently, the sentence 'Following such transfer' becomes contradictory because the transfer is now discretionary and may not occur, which makes the Licensee responsibility for maintaining regulatory approvals inconsistent with the earlier statement that there's no obligation for a transfer to ever occur. This creates a significant conflict within the text, rendering the provision unclear and potentially unenforceable as the condition for Licensee's obligation to maintain such licenses is predicated on Bioeq transferring them, yet Bioeq is under no obligation to do so.
